displayTitle: The Flaw That Draws Its Own Picture
kicker: MT LEVEL I // FROM HOKE TO TODAY

beats.narration: Welcome to magnetic particle testing, the N D T method where the flaw shows itself, right on the part.

payload.title: MAGNETIC PARTICLE
payload.sub: The flaw draws its own picture, right on the part.

beats.narration: Eighteen sixty-eight: Saxby's compass stirs over flawed gun barrels. Nineteen eighteen: Hoke's grindings gather on cracks. Nineteen thirty-four: de Forest and Doane take it industrial.
The principle Hoke stumbled onto: magnetize the part, and a crack forces flux out of the surface. Particles pile up right at the leak.

payload.heading: Leakage flux marks the crack
payload.note: CRACK → FLUX LEAKS OUT → PARTICLES COLLECT

beats.narration: MT's lane: surface and near-surface flaws in ferromagnetic steel and iron. Aluminum or austenitic stainless? MT does nothing; reach for penetrant or eddy current.

payload.heading: MT's lane — and where it ends

beats.narration: So keep this rule in your pocket: magnet won't stick, MT won't see. Know each method's lane and pick the right tool.

payload.sub: Know each method's lane — pick the right tool.
